chunks.text: ORDER SHEET THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH, CIRCUIT COURT LARKANA C. P No.S-333 of 2025 Date Order with signature of Judge 1. For orders on office objections ‘A’. 2. For hearing of main case. 13-10-2025 Mr. Aijaz Ali Chandio, advocate for the petitioner. Mr. Liaquat Ali Shar, A.A.G. Mr. Zain-ul-Abideen Abbasi, A.P.G. ************** Learned counsel for the petitioner submits that the petitioner contracted marriage with Mst. Ayesha on 10.01.2025, after her suit for dissolution of marriage was decreed by the learned Family Judge, Mehar. He contends that respondents No.5 to 14, being aggrieved by the said marriage, have been issuing threats of murder on pretext of Karo Kari, allegedly at the instigation of respondent No.11. He further submits that the petitioner and his wife had earlier approached the learned Sessions Judge/Justice of Peace, Naushero Feroz, seeking protection, whereupon their application was disposed of with directions to provide them legal protection. It is further submitted that the petitioner and his wife are presently residing in District Dadu, where respondent No.2, allegedly at the behest of private respondents, has been harassing the petitioner and his family. The petitioner accordingly prays for protection. Learned Additional Advocate General appears and files comments on behalf of respondents No.2 and 4, which are taken on record. Respondent No.2, in his comments, denies the allegations of harassment and states that no such action has been taken against the petitioner; however, he affirms that legal protection shall be provided to the petitioner in accordance with law. Learned A.A.G further submits that the official respondents are duty bound to act strictly in accordance with law and to ensure the safety and protection of the petitioner and his wife.

Respondent No.2, in his comments, denies the allegations of harassment and states that no such action has been taken against the petitioner; however, he affirms that legal protection shall be provided to the petitioner in accordance with law. Learned A.A.G further submits that the official respondents are duty bound to act strictly in accordance with law and to ensure the safety and protection of the petitioner and his wife. Learned counsel for the petitioner, being satisfied with the comments filed on behalf of respondents No.2 and 4, does not press the petition and seeks its disposal. In view of the above, the petition is hereby disposed of, with directions to the official respondents to act strictly in accordance with law and to provide legal protection to the petitioner and his wife as and when required. Let a copy of this order be transmitted to the office of the learned Additional Advocate General as well as the official respondents for information and compliance. Judge Irshad Ali M/Steno
